items.title: 'Big polluting companies like Total have climate obligations under French law'
items.description: Genie Godula is pleased to welcome Justine Ripoll, Campaign Manager for French NGO "Notre Affaire à Tous", and one of four NGOs who brought the climate case against Total Energy. She argues that climate litigation not as a symbolic confrontation with the fossil fuel industry, but as an evolving mechanism of governance that seeks to clarify and enforce corporate responsibility under climate law. Ripoll shifts the conversation from technological inevitability to political and ethical prioritisation, arguing that the remaining carbon budget should be allocated according to collective social needs rather than commercial interests. She asserts that multinational energy companies can no longer position climate change solely as a matter for governments. Instead, she contends that businesses whose activities substantially contribute to greenhouse gas emissions carry independent legal obligations that courts are increasingly willing to recognise.

items.description: In this edition of French Connections, Genie Godula and Florence Villeminot continue their tour of Paris with a stop in the 10th arrondissement of Paris. Located in the heart of the Right Bank, it’s one of the most vibrant parts of the capital. A lot of people enter Paris through the dixième, thanks to the city’s two biggest train stations, the Gare de Nord et Gare de l’Est. They, along with the much-loved Canal Saint Martin, have helped turn the 10th into a multicultural melting pot of restaurants, bars, theatres and other hidden gems that capture the essence of real Parisian life.

items.title: Why Austrian author Ingeborg Bachmann remains a literary icon

items.title: Social infrastructure during heatwave: 'Knock on the door makes the difference between life & death'
items.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Eric Klinenberg, Professor of Sociology at NYU and Author of "Heat Wave: A Social Autopsy of Disaster in Chicago". He challenges one of the dominant assumptions surrounding extreme heat: that technological adaptation alone can protect societies from climate change. Drawing on his landmark study of Chicago's deadly 1995 heatwave, he argues that mortality during extreme heat is shaped as much by the strength of social ties as by meteorological conditions.

items.title: ‘Like working in a kettle’: France’s overcrowded prisons swelter under historic heatwave
items.description: The deadly heatwave sweeping France has once again exposed the structural problems of the country’s chronically overcrowded prisons, with groups of three or four detainees crammed into airless cells built to hold a lone prisoner. Analysts and prison staff alike have criticised a penal system that continues to see mass incarceration as the main means of cracking down on crime.

items.title: Plan Intl: ‘Mental health impact of Venezuelan quakes can last for years’
items.description: Plan International said children and young people must be at the centre of Venezuela's earthquake response.

items.title: Venezuela declares state of emergency after powerful twin earthquakes
items.description: Two massive earthquakes of magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 struck Venezuela on Wednesday evening; It remains unknown whether the incident caused fatalities, but some buildings collapsed and residents were driven into the streets in panic. Leer más

items.title: Poland, Ukraine hold key Gdansk conference without Zelenskyy
items.description: Polish PM Donald Tusk called on the West to believe a post-war Ukraine will “rise from the ruins” like his native city Gdansk, largely destroyed during World War II, as he tried to dampen the diplomatic spat with Kyiv

items.title: Trading Day: Tech outlook is cloudy
World Health Organization announces trial of two potential Ebola treatments
items.description: They will begin next week in the DR Congo to see whether two antivirals can help reduce mortality in patients with the rare Bundibugyo strain of the virus.

items.title: Another suspected H5 bird flu case detected in WA
items.description: Another suspected positive case of the H5 bird flu has been detected in Western Australia, marking the fifth confirmed or suspected case in Australia.

items.title: Consider Teesta water sharing deal under ‘current circumstances’: Bangladesh FM post BJP’s Bengal win
items.description: Mamata percieved as hurdle in Teesta talks; ahead of Beijing trip, Bangladesh FM says, “Of course, Teesta will be discussed with China”; Dhaka demands greater share of Teesta waters

items.title: Japan draw 1-1 with Sweden at World Cup to finish second in Group F
items.description: Goals from Daizen Maeda and Anthony Elanga were enough to see both teams through to the Round of 32.

items.title: Venezuela earthquake death toll rises to 235
items.description: The number of dead and injured is expected to rise with thousands reported missing

items.title: UN agency pauses Hormuz ship evacuation initiative after vessel attacked
items.description: Iran warns vessels to use only Tehran-approved routes as IMO halts Hormuz evacuation after ship struck.

items.title: Earthquake is devastating blow to Venezuela at time of uncertainty
items.description: The twin quakes come less than six months after Venezuela's then-leader Nicolás Maduro was seized by US forces.

items.title: Why is Germany taking a big stake in a major tankmaker?
items.description: The German government will buy a 40% stake in tankmaker KNDS. The deal is as much about control in the critical defense sector as it is about the Franco-German relationship.

items.title: Heat and drinks breaks help smaller teams at World Cup, says Argentina coach Scaloni
items.description: Hydration breaks are helping the smaller teams at the World Cup, Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni said Sunday. Leer más

items.title: Cargo ship reports suspected attack while crossing Hormuz
items.description: The vessel hit was the Singapore-flagged container ship Ever Lovely, according to British maritime risk management group Vanguard and three maritime security sources

items.title: World Cup: South Africa reaches knockouts with win over Korea
items.description: South Africa advanced to the knockout phase of the World Cup for the first time with a 1-0 victory over South Korea on Wednesday night

items.title: HRW says Cameroon not doing enough to combat violence against women
items.description: In a new report, it said violence against women and girls remains widespread despite repeated government promises to do something about it.

items.title: Watch: Moment earthquakes hit airport and buildings in Caracas
items.description: Video filmed by BBC reporters, contributors and locals shows the aftermath, as frantic residents evacuate.

items.title: Lionel Scaloni on Argentina's win over Austria: 'When Messi switches on, everyone switches on'
items.description: Lionel Messi scored both goals in Argentina's 2-0 victory over Austria, securing the Albiceleste's place in the knockout stages of the 2026 World Cup. Leer más

items.title: Afghanistan's Taliban government imposes smartphone ban on government officials
items.description: Rights advocates said smartphones remain ⁠one of the few tools Afghans use to access education, document abuses, communicate privately and reach uncensored information

items.title: UN adopts resolution upholding accountability for attacks on peacekeepers
items.description: It authorises new steps to ensure that those who commit crimes against peacekeepers face justice.

items.title: Tunisia upholds eight-year jail term for anti-racism activist
items.description: A Tunisian court on Tuesday upheld an eight-year prison sentence slapped on anti-racism activist Saadia Mosbah for financial misconduct, two of her lawyers said

items.title: Trump's 'Alligator Alcatraz' detention center closes after a year of controversy
items.description: Florida's controversial "Alligator Alcatraz" immigration detention centre, a symbol of US President Donald Trump's deportation drive, has closed after operating for less than a year, officials said on Thursday. Florida Governor Ron DeSantis said the Everglades facility had emptied after fulfilling its emergency role despite criticism over detainees' conditions and due process.

items.title: Stefanovic's move to outsider politics follows a well-worn lucrative path
items.description: Listening to the culture war showman schtick he has so quickly adopted, Karl Stefanovic is now less a newsman than an ideological performer.

items.title: Oil prices climb after attack in Strait of Hormuz halts evacuation plan
items.description: Brent crude rises after cargo ship comes under attack in key waterway.

items.title: North Korea: Kim Jong Un oversees ballistic missile tests, calls for stronger 'offensive posture'
items.description: Pyongyang tested "major weapons" as North Korea's leader called for greater strike capabilities along the border with South Korea.

items.title: Two men charged as NSW Police seize 64 kilograms of  drugs
items.description: Police have charged two men and seized almost 64 kilograms of drugs with an estimated street value of more than $2.5 million, north of Newcastle.

items.title: Stellantis, Nissan eye Marelli assets in rescue talks, Bloomberg reports
US Supreme Court sides with Bayer over Roundup cancer suits
items.description: Bayer, which acquired Roundup in 2018, said the decision is good for science and farmers. The Supreme Court's ruling was met with backlash from both Republicans and Democrats.

items.title: US seeks Iran deal, but 'not at any price,' Rubio says
items.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said on Thursday Washington would not accept any Iran deal allowing Tehran to impose fees on shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, warning it would cause "total chaos." His remarks came after an attack on a vessel forced the United Nations to suspend mariner evacuation efforts.

items.title: Supreme Court allows Trump to end protected status for Haitian and Syrian immigrants
items.description: The ruling opens the path for the Trump administration to deport hundreds of thousands of immigrants who have been living in the US for years.

items.title: Exclusive-Bumble dating app explores sale, sources say
FATF asks Myanmar to act against cyber scam threats, protect trafficking victims
items.description: The FATF noted that fraud and cyber scam activities in Myanmar remained extensive and presented significant illicit finance risks, despite steps taken to tackle such operations

items.title: Turkey arrests more than 200 in crackdown before NATO summit
items.description: The Ankara prosecutor's office said 209 people were detained, while 32 others remained at large. Turkey has announced a ban on demonstrations amid preparations for next month's NATO summit in the capital.

items.title: WNBA: Alyssa Thomas suspended one game for fist into Cailtin Clark’s throat
items.description: Both players were on the floor when Thomas pushed her fist into Clark's throat before getting up and stepping over her.

items.title: When do the Socceroos play next and who will Australia play?
items.description: Australia has secured its placed in the knockout stages of the World Cup — but who will they play and when?

items.title: Germany news: Major warship project to be scrapped
items.description: The Defense Ministry is dropping a multi-billion-euro project to build F126 frigates, media say. And train services have resumed after a communications glitch overnight halted Germany's rail network . DW has more.

items.title: Iran: UK navy reports cargo vessel hit in Strait of Hormuz
items.description: The reported attack has caused the UN maritime agency to halt its evacuations of seafarers stranded in the Gulf. Meanwhile, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio is finishing up his Mideast tour.

items.title: Honda CEO apologises for company's loss, wins investor backing at annual meeting

items.title: Trump ally wins Colombia election, heralding pro-market turn
items.description: Election of conservative lawyer heralds swing back to business-friendly and pro-US policies after four years of leftist rule under Gustavo Petro. Leer más

items.title: A Chinese box office hit sparks a debate about identity in Singapore
items.description: A nostalgic tale about family, hope and hardship has opened an unexpected conversation.
